fr.elements: Main Building; Detached Structure; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Enhancing responder access and operational efficiency around the property could be facilitated by ensuring both structural and vehicular access points are well-marked and easily navigable. Providing clear pathways through the landscaped area and maintaining unobstructed routes in the open green space can support both movement and staging for emergency personnel. Installing proper signage at the front driveway and any entry points to delineate access routes and structure orientations could assist in navigation and rapid response activities. Additional rear access pathways or routes around the structures may enhance responder entry and exit capabilities during emergency operations, reducing response times and improving safety for both responders and property occupants.

wildfire.conditions: Tree Cluster; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Scattered Shrubs; Defensible Space; Open Terrain; Vegetated Perimeter; Vegetated Perimeter
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by maintaining clear separation between the tree cluster and nearby structures, as well as managing vegetation directly adjacent to structures, could reduce the pathway for fire to spread to the buildings. Scattered shrubs should be evaluated and selectively thinned to break up fuel continuity, particularly ensuring they are not in proximity to structural features. The existing open terrain provides a potential buffer against fire spread; maintaining this openness and reducing vegetation density at the perimeters could help limit the intensity and reach of a wildfire. Vegetation along the property's perimeter should be strategically managed to create fuel breaks, supporting both property protection and long-term wildfire resilience. Ensuring the access routes remain clear of overgrown vegetation will facilitate firefighter ingress and egress, further assisting suppression efforts if needed.
